{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Sakuradani Kofun Cluster | native_name = 桜谷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakuradani Kofun Group (Takaoka)-2, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakuradani Kofun No.2 | map_type = Japan Toyama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|59.28|N|137|1|51.82|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Takaoka, Toyama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sakuradani Kofun Cluster'''|桜谷古墳群| Sakuradani Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ōta neighborhood of the city of [[Takaoka, Toyama]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. Two of the tumuli were designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216289 |title= 桜谷古墳群 |trans-title= Sakuradani kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakuradani Kofun Cluster of ''kofun'' is located on the margin of a hill at the base of Mount Futagami overlooking [[Toyama Bay]] in northwestern Toyama Prefecture. The site consisted of a ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) tumuli (No.1), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above and a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hotategaigata-kofun''|帆立貝型古墳||}}) tumuli (No.2) and ten or more smaller {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}) tumuli. The first was discovered in 1918 when a tree was being planted at a local [[Shinto shrine]], and nine more were discovered by 1924. Tombs No.1 and No.2 have not been surveyed internally in detail, but are presumed to date from the beginning of the fifth century in the late [[Kofun period]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These two tombs were protected as a National Historic Site since 1934; however, the remaining tombs were destroyed when the area was developed for agricultural land after [[World War II]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
In 1976, one more dome-shaped tumulus and two box-shaped stone sarcophagus were discovered during the construction of a prefectural road. However, during this same construction work and despite the National Historic Site status, a portion of Tomb No.2 was destroyed.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Numerous [[grave good]]s uncovered during various [[archaeological excavations]], of which a 14-centimeter diameter [[bronze mirror]] (possibly of Chinese origin), 13 cylindrical beads, three gold rings, two knives, 17 small beads and a jasper spindle are designated as Important Tangible Cultural Properties of Takaoka City. The mirror is kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and other artifacts at the Toyama City Museum. The site is about a 10-minute walk from [[Amaharashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Himi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Two of the tumuli were designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216289 |title= 桜谷古墳群 |trans-title= Sakuradani kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakuradani Kofun Cluster of ''kofun'' is located on the margin of a hill at the base of Mount Futagami overlooking [[Toyama Bay]] in northwestern Toyama Prefecture. The site consisted of a ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) tumuli (No.1), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above and a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hotategaigata-kofun''|帆立貝型古墳||}}) tumuli (No.2) and ten or more smaller {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}) tumuli. The first was discovered in 1918 when a tree was being planted at a local [[Shinto shrine]], and nine more were discovered by 1924. Tombs No.1 and No.2 have not been surveyed internally in detail, but are presumed to date from the beginning of the fifth century in the late [[Kofun period]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These two tombs were protected as a National Historic Site since 1934; however, the remaining tombs were destroyed when the area was developed for agricultural land after [[World War II]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
In 1976, one more dome-shaped tumulus and two box-shaped stone sarcophagus were discovered during the construction of a prefectural road. However, during this same construction work and despite the National Historic Site status, a portion of Tomb No.2 was destroyed.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" |+ Sakuradani Kofun cluster |- ! Kofun !! type !! Length || Posterior || Anterior || Excavated |- | No.1 || keyhole || 62m || 35m diameter x 7m height || 30m width x 5.45 m height || unexcavated |- | No.2 || keyhole || 50m || 33m diameter x 6m height || ?m width x 1 m height || 1923, 1945 |- |}
Numerous [[grave good]]s uncovered during various [[archaeological excavations]], of which a 14-centimeter diameter [[bronze mirror]] (possibly of Chinese origin), 13 cylindrical beads, three gold rings, two knives, 17 small beads and a jasper spindle are designated as Important Tangible Cultural Properties of Takaoka City. The mirror is kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and other artifacts at the Toyama City Museum. The site is about a 10-minute walk from [[Amaharashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Himi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Two of the tumuli were designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216289 |title= 桜谷古墳群 |trans-title= Sakuradani kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakuradani Kofun Cluster of ''kofun'' is located on the margin of a hill at the base of Mount Futagami overlooking [[Toyama Bay]] in northwestern Toyama Prefecture. The first was discovered in 1918 when a tree was being planted at a local [[Shinto shrine]], and nine more were discovered by 1924. Tombs No.1 and No.2 have not been surveyed internally in detail, but are presumed to date from the beginning of the fifth century in the late [[Kofun period]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Sakuradani Kofun Cluster | native_name = 桜谷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakuradani Kofun Group (Takaoka)-2, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakuradani Kofun No.2 | map_type = Japan Toyama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|59.28|N|137|1|51.82|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Takaoka, Toyama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sakuradani Kofun Cluster'''|桜谷古墳群| Sakuradani Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ōta neighborhood of the city of [[Takaoka, Toyama]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. Two of the tumuli were designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216289 |title= 桜谷古墳群 |trans-title= Sakuradani kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakuradani Kofun Cluster of ''kofun'' is located on the margin of a hill at the base of Mount Futagami overlooking [[Toyama Bay]] in northwestern Toyama Prefecture. The site consisted of a ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) tumuli (No.1), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above and a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hotategaigata-kofun''|帆立貝型古墳||}}) tumuli (No.2) and ten or more smaller {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}) tumuli. The first was discovered in 1918 when a tree was being planted at a local [[Shinto shrine]], and nine more were discovered by 1924. Tombs No.1 and No.2 have not been surveyed internally in detail, but are presumed to date from the beginning of the fifth century in the late [[Kofun period]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These two tombs were protected as a National Historic Site since 1934; however, the remaining tombs were destroyed when the area was developed for agricultural land after [[World War II]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
In 1976, one more dome-shaped tumulus and two box-shaped stone sarcophagus were discovered during the construction of a prefectural road. However, during this same construction work and despite the National Historic Site status, a portion of Tomb No.2 was destroyed.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" |+ Sakuradani Kofun cluster |- ! Kofun !! type !! Length || Posterior || Anterior || Excavated |- | No.1 || keyhole || 62m || 35m diameter x 7m height || 30m width x 5.45 m height || unexcavated |- | No.2 || keyhole || 50m || 33m diameter x 6m height || ?m width x 1 m height || 1923, 1945 |- |}
Numerous [[grave good]]s uncovered during various [[archaeological excavations]], of which a 14-centimeter diameter [[bronze mirror]] (possibly of Chinese origin), 13 cylindrical beads, three gold rings, two knives, 17 small beads and a jasper spindle are designated as Important Tangible Cultural Properties of Takaoka City. The mirror is kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and other artifacts at the Toyama City Museum. The site is about a 10-minute walk from [[Amaharashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Himi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Sakuradani Kofun Cluster | native_name = 桜谷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakuradani Kofun Group (Takaoka)-2, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakuradani Kofun No.2 | map_type = Japan Toyama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|59.28|N|137|1|51.82|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Takaoka, Toyama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sakuradani Kofun Cluster'''|桜谷古墳群| Sakuradani Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ōta neighborhood of the city of [[Takaoka, Toyama]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. Two of the tumuli were designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216289 |title= 桜谷古墳群 |trans-title= Sakuradani kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakuradani Kofun Cluster of ''kofun'' is located on the margin of a hill at the base of Mount Futagami overlooking [[Toyama Bay]] in northwestern Toyama Prefecture. The site consisted of a ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) tumuli (No.1), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above and a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hotategaigata-kofun''|帆立貝型古墳||}}) tumuli (No.2) and ten or more smaller {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}) tumuli. The first was discovered in 1918 when a tree was being planted at a local [[Shinto shrine]], and nine more were discovered by 1924. Tombs No.1 and No.2 have not been surveyed internally in detail, but are presumed to date from the beginning of the fifth century in the late [[Kofun period]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These two tombs were protected as a National Historic Site since 1934; however, the remaining tombs were destroyed when the area was developed for agricultural land after [[World War II]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
In 1976, one more dome-shaped tumulus and two box-shaped stone sarcophagus were discovered during the construction of a prefectural road. However, during this same construction work and despite the National Historic Site status, a portion of Tomb No.2 was destroyed.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" |+ Sakuradani Kofun cluster |- ! Kofun !! type !! Length || Posterior || Anterior || Excavated |- | No.1 || keyhole || 62m || 35m diameter x 7m height || 30m width x 5.45 m height || unexcavated |- | No.2 || keyhole || 50m || 33m diameter x 6m height || ?m width x 1 m height || 1923, 1945 |- |}
Numerous [[grave good]]s uncovered during various [[archaeological excavations]], of which a 14-centimeter diameter [[bronze mirror]] (possibly of Chinese origin), 13 cylindrical beads, three gold rings, two knives, 17 small beads and a jasper spindle are designated as Important Tangible Cultural Properties of Takaoka City. The mirror is kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and other artifacts at the Toyama City Museum. The site is about a 10-minute walk from [[Amaharashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Himi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Toyama)]]

==Overview== The Sakuradani Kofun Cluster of ''kofun'' is located on the margin of a hill at the base of Mount Futagami overlooking [[Toyama Bay]] in northwestern Toyama Prefecture. The site consisted of a ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) tumuli (No.1), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above and a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hotategaigata-kofun''|帆立貝型古墳||}}) tumuli (No.2) and ten or more smaller {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}) tumuli. The first was discovered in 1918 when a tree was being planted at a local [[Shinto shrine]], and nine more were discovered by 1924. Tombs No.1 and No.2 have not been surveyed internally in detail, but are presumed to date from the beginning of the fifth century in the late [[Kofun period]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These two tombs were protected as a National Historic Site since 1934; however, the remaining tombs were destroyed when the area was developed for agricultural land after [[World War II]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The mirror is kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and other artifacts at the Toyama City Museum. The site is about a 10-minute walk from [[Amaharashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Himi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Sakuradani Kofun Cluster | native_name = 桜谷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakuradani Kofun Group (Takaoka)-2, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakuradani Kofun No.2 | map_type = Japan Toyama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|59.28|N|137|1|51.82|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Takaoka, Toyama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sakuradani Kofun Cluster'''|桜谷古墳群| Sakuradani Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ōta neighborhood of the city of [[Takaoka, Toyama]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. Two of the tumuli were designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216289 |title= 桜谷古墳群 |trans-title= Sakuradani kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakuradani Kofun Cluster of ''kofun'' is located on the margin of a hill at the base of Mount Futagami overlooking [[Toyama Bay]] in northwestern Toyama Prefecture. The site consisted of a ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) tumuli (No.1), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above and a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hotategaigata-kofun''|帆立貝型古墳||}}) tumuli (No.2) and ten or more smaller {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}) tumuli. The first was discovered in 1918 when a tree was being planted at a local [[Shinto shrine]], and nine more were discovered by 1924. Tombs No.1 and No.2 have not been surveyed internally in detail, but are presumed to date from the beginning of the fifth century in the late [[Kofun period]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These two tombs were protected as a National Historic Site since 1934; however, the remaining tombs were destroyed when the area was developed for agricultural land after [[World War II]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
In 1976, one more dome-shaped tumulus and two box-shaped stone sarcophagus were discovered during the construction of a prefectural road. However, during this same construction work and despite the National Historic Site status, a portion of Tomb No.2 was destroyed.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" |+ Sakuradani Kofun cluster |- ! Kofun !! type !! Length || Posterior || Anterior || Excavated |- | No.1 || keyhole || 62m || 35m diameter x 7m height || 30m width x 5.45 m height || unexcavated |- | No.2 || keyhole || 50m || 33m diameter x 6m height || ?m width x 1 m height || 1923, 1945 |- |}
Numerous [[grave good]]s uncovered during various [[archaeological excavations]], of which a 14-centimeter diameter [[bronze mirror]] (possibly of Chinese origin), 13 cylindrical beads, three gold rings, two knives, 17 small beads and a jasper spindle are designated as Important Tangible Cultural Properties of Takaoka City. The mirror is kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and other artifacts at the Toyama City Museum. The site is about a 10-minute walk from [[Amaharashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Himi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Sakuradani Kofun Cluster | native_name = 桜谷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakuradani Kofun Group (Takaoka)-2, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakuradani Kofun No.2 | map_type = Japan Toyama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|59.28|N|137|1|51.82|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Takaoka, Toyama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sakuradani Kofun Cluster'''|桜谷古墳群| Sakuradani Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ōta neighborhood of the city of [[Takaoka, Toyama]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. Two of the tumuli were designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216289 |title= 桜谷古墳群 |trans-title= Sakuradani kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakuradani Kofun Cluster of ''kofun'' is located on the margin of a hill at the base of Mount Futagami overlooking [[Toyama Bay]] in northwestern Toyama Prefecture. The site consisted of a ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) tumuli (No.1), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above and a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''hotategaigata-kofun''|帆立貝型古墳||}}) tumuli (No.2) and ten or more smaller {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}) tumuli. The first was discovered in 1918 when a tree was being planted at a local [[Shinto shrine]], and nine more were discovered by 1924. Tombs No.1 and No.2 have not been surveyed internally in detail, but are presumed to date from the beginning of the fifth century in the late [[Kofun period]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These two tombs were protected as a National Historic Site since 1934; however, the remaining tombs were destroyed when the area was developed for agricultural land after [[World War II]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
In 1976, one more dome-shaped tumulus and two box-shaped stone sarcophagus were discovered during the construction of a prefectural road. However, during this same construction work and despite the National Historic Site status, a portion of Tomb No.2 was destroyed.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" |+ Sakuradani Kofun cluster |- ! Kofun !! type !! Length || Posterior || Anterior || Excavated |- | No.1 || keyhole || 62m || 35m diameter x 7m height || 30m width x 5.45 m height || unexcavated |- | No.2 || keyhole || 50m || 33m diameter x 6m height || ?m width x 1 m height || 1923, 1945 |- |}
Numerous [[grave good]]s uncovered during various [[archaeological excavations]], of which a 14-centimeter diameter [[bronze mirror]] (possibly of Chinese origin), 13 cylindrical beads, three gold rings, two knives, 17 small beads and a jasper spindle are designated as Important Tangible Cultural Properties of Takaoka City. The mirror is kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and other artifacts at the Toyama City Museum. The site is about a 10-minute walk from [[Amaharashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Himi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} | name = Sakuradani Kofun Cluster | native_name = 桜谷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakuradani Kofun Group (Takaoka)-2, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakuradani Kofun No.2 | map_type = Japan Toyama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|59.28|N|137|1|51.82|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Takaoka, Toyama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sakuradani Kofun Cluster'''|桜谷古墳群| Sakuradani Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ōta neighborhood of the city of [[Takaoka, Toyama]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. Two of the tumuli were designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216289 |title= 桜谷古墳群 |trans-title= Sakuradani kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakuradani Kofun Cluster of ''kofun'' is located on the margin of a hill at the base of Mount Futagami overlooking [[Toyama Bay]] in northwestern Toyama Prefecture. The site consisted of a ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) tumuli (No.1), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above and a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} ({{nihongo|''hotategaigata-kofun''|帆立貝型古墳||}}) tumuli (No.2) and ten or more smaller {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}) tumuli. The first was discovered in 1918 when a tree was being planted at a local [[Shinto shrine]], and nine more were discovered by 1924. Tombs No.1 and No.2 have not been surveyed internally in detail, but are presumed to date from the beginning of the fifth century in the late [[Kofun period]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These two tombs were protected as a National Historic Site since 1934; however, the remaining tombs were destroyed when the area was developed for agricultural land after [[World War II]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
In 1976, one more dome-shaped tumulus and two box-shaped stone sarcophagus were discovered during the construction of a prefectural road. However, during this same construction work and despite the National Historic Site status, a portion of Tomb No.2 was destroyed.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" |+ Sakuradani Kofun cluster |- ! Kofun !! type !! Length || Posterior || Anterior || Excavated |- | No.1 || keyhole || 62m || 35m diameter x 7m height || 30m width x 5.45 m height || unexcavated |- | No.2 || keyhole || 50m || 33m diameter x 6m height || ?m width x 1 m height || 1923, 1945 |- |}
Numerous [[grave good]]s uncovered during various [[archaeological excavations]], of which a 14-centimeter diameter [[bronze mirror]] (possibly of Chinese origin), 13 cylindrical beads, three gold rings, two knives, 17 small beads and a jasper spindle are designated as Important Tangible Cultural Properties of Takaoka City. The mirror is kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and other artifacts at the Toyama City Museum. The site is about a 10-minute walk from [[Amaharashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Himi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Toyama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Sakuradani Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.takaoka.toyama.jp/syoubun/kanko/rekishi/kinen/sakuradani.html Takaoka city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.pref.toyama.jp/sections/3009/3007/mb/mb002-23.htm Toyama Prefectural home page] {{in lang|ja}}
Sakuradani Kofun

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} | name = Sakuradani Kofun Cluster | native_name = 桜谷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakuradani Kofun Group (Takaoka)-2, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakuradani Kofun No.2 | map_type = Japan Toyama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|59.28|N|137|1|51.82|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Takaoka, Toyama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sakuradani Kofun Cluster'''|桜谷古墳群| Sakuradani Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ōta neighborhood of the city of [[Takaoka, Toyama]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. Two of the tumuli were designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216289 |title= 桜谷古墳群 |trans-title= Sakuradani kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakuradani Kofun Cluster of ''kofun'' is located on the margin of a hill at the base of Mount Futagami overlooking [[Toyama Bay]] in northwestern Toyama Prefecture. The site consisted of a ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) tumuli (No.1), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above and a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} ({{nihongo|''hotategaigata-kofun''|帆立貝型古墳||}}) tumuli (No.2) and ten or more smaller {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}) tumuli. The first was discovered in 1918 when a tree was being planted at a local [[Shinto shrine]], and nine more were discovered by 1924. Tombs No.1 and No.2 have not been surveyed internally in detail, but are presumed to date from the beginning of the fifth century in the late [[Kofun period]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These two tombs were protected as a National Historic Site since 1934; however, the remaining tombs were destroyed when the area was developed for agricultural land after [[World War II]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
In 1976, one more dome-shaped tumulus and two box-shaped stone sarcophagus were discovered during the construction of a prefectural road. However, during this same construction work and despite the National Historic Site status, a portion of Tomb No.2 was destroyed.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" |+ Sakuradani Kofun cluster |- ! Kofun !! type !! Length || Posterior || Anterior || Excavated |- | No.1 || keyhole || 62m || 35m diameter x 7m height || 30m width x 5.45 m height || unexcavated |- | No.2 || keyhole || 50m || 33m diameter x 6m height || ?m width x 1 m height || 1923, 1945 |- |}
Numerous [[grave good]]s uncovered during various [[archaeological excavations]], of which a 14-centimeter diameter [[bronze mirror]] (possibly of Chinese origin), 13 cylindrical beads, three gold rings, two knives, 17 small beads and a jasper spindle are designated as Important Tangible Cultural Properties of Takaoka City. The mirror is kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and other artifacts at the Toyama City Museum. The site is about a 10-minute walk from [[Amaharashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Himi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Toyama)]]

Two of the tumuli were designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216289 |title= 桜谷古墳群 |trans-title= Sakuradani kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakuradani Kofun Cluster of ''kofun'' is located on the margin of a hill at the base of Mount Futagami overlooking [[Toyama Bay]] in northwestern Toyama Prefecture. The site consisted of a ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) tumuli (No.1), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above and a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} ({{nihongo|''hotategaigata-kofun''|帆立貝型古墳||}}) tumuli (No.2) and ten or more smaller {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}) tumuli. The first was discovered in 1918 when a tree was being planted at a local [[Shinto shrine]], and nine more were discovered by 1924. Tombs No.1 and No.2 have not been surveyed internally in detail, but are presumed to date from the beginning of the fifth century in the late [[Kofun period]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These two tombs were protected as a National Historic Site since 1934; however, the remaining tombs were destroyed when the area was developed for agricultural land after [[World War II]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
In 1976, one more dome-shaped tumulus and two box-shaped stone sarcophagus were discovered during the construction of a prefectural road. However, during this same construction work and despite the National Historic Site status, a portion of Tomb No.2 was destroyed.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" |+ Sakuradani Kofun cluster |- ! Kofun !! type !! Length || Posterior || Anterior || Excavated |- | No.1 || keyhole || 62m || 35m diameter x 7m height || 30m width x 5.45 m height || unexcavated |- | No.2 || keyhole || 50m || 33m diameter x 6m height || ?m width x 1 m height || 1923, 1945 |- |}
Numerous [[grave good]]s uncovered during various [[archaeological excavations]], of which a 14-centimeter diameter [[bronze mirror]] (possibly of Chinese origin), 13 cylindrical beads, three gold rings, two knives, 17 small beads and a jasper spindle are designated as Important Tangible Cultural Properties of Takaoka City. The mirror is kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and other artifacts at the Toyama City Museum. The site is about a 10-minute walk from [[Amaharashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Himi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The site consisted of a ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) tumuli (No.1), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above and a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} ({{nihongo|''hotategaigata-kofun''|帆立貝型古墳||}}) tumuli (No.2) and ten or more smaller {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}) tumuli. The first was discovered in 1918 when a tree was being planted at a local [[Shinto shrine]], and nine more were discovered by 1924. Tombs No.1 and No.2 have not been surveyed internally in detail, but are presumed to date from the beginning of the fifth century in the late [[Kofun period]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These two tombs were protected as a National Historic Site since 1934; however, the remaining tombs were destroyed when the area was developed for agricultural land after [[World War II]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
In 1976, one more dome-shaped tumulus and two box-shaped stone sarcophagus were discovered during the construction of a prefectural road. However, during this same construction work and despite the National Historic Site status, a portion of Tomb No.2 was destroyed.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" |+ Sakuradani Kofun cluster |- ! Kofun !! type !! Length || Posterior || Anterior || Excavated |- | No.1 || keyhole || 62m || 35m diameter x 7m height || 30m width x 5.45 m height || unexcavated |- | No.2 || keyhole || 50m || 33m diameter x 6m height || ?m width x 1 m height || 1923, 1945 |- |}
Numerous [[grave good]]s uncovered during various [[archaeological excavations]], of which a 14-centimeter diameter [[bronze mirror]] (possibly of Chinese origin), 13 cylindrical beads, three gold rings, two knives, 17 small beads and a jasper spindle are designated as Important Tangible Cultural Properties of Takaoka City. The mirror is kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and other artifacts at the Toyama City Museum. The site is about a 10-minute walk from [[Amaharashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Himi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Sakuradani Kofun Cluster | native_name = 桜谷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakuradani Kofun Group (Takaoka)-2, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakuradani Kofun No.2 | map_type = Japan Toyama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|59.28|N|137|1|51.82|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Takaoka, Toyama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sakuradani Kofun Cluster'''|桜谷古墳群| Sakuradani Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ōta neighborhood of the city of [[Takaoka, Toyama]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. Two of the tumuli were designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216289 |title= 桜谷古墳群 |trans-title= Sakuradani kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakuradani Kofun Cluster of ''kofun'' is located on the margin of a hill at the base of Mount Futagami overlooking [[Toyama Bay]] in northwestern Toyama Prefecture. The site consisted of a ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) tumuli (No.1), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above and a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hotategaigata-kofun''|帆立貝型古墳||}}) tumuli (No.2) and ten or more smaller {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}) tumuli. The first was discovered in 1918 when a tree was being planted at a local [[Shinto shrine]], and nine more were discovered by 1924. Tombs No.1 and No.2 have not been surveyed internally in detail, but are presumed to date from the beginning of the fifth century in the late [[Kofun period]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These two tombs were protected as a National Historic Site since 1934; however, the remaining tombs were destroyed when the area was developed for agricultural land after [[World War II]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
In 1976, one more dome-shaped tumulus and two box-shaped stone sarcophagus were discovered during the construction of a prefectural road. However, during this same construction work and despite the National Historic Site status, a portion of Tomb No.2 was destroyed.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" |+ Sakuradani Kofun cluster |- ! Kofun !! type !! Length || Posterior || Anterior || Excavated |- | No.1 || keyhole || 62m || 35m diameter x 7m height || 30m width x 5.45 m height || unexcavated |- | No.2 || keyhole || 50m || 33m diameter x 6m height || ?m width x 1 m height || 1923, 1945 |- |}
Numerous [[grave good]]s uncovered during various [[archaeological excavations]], of which a 14-centimeter diameter [[bronze mirror]] (possibly of Chinese origin), 13 cylindrical beads, three gold rings, two knives, 17 small beads and a jasper spindle are designated as Important Tangible Cultural Properties of Takaoka City. The mirror is kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and other artifacts at the Toyama City Museum. The site is about a 10-minute walk from [[Amaharashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Himi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Toyama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Sakuradani Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.takaoka.toyama.jp/syoubun/kanko/rekishi/kinen/sakuradani.html Takaoka city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.pref.toyama.jp/sections/3009/3007/mb/mb002-23.htm Toyama Prefectural home page] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Sakuradani Kofun Cluster | native_name = 桜谷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakuradani Kofun Group (Takaoka)-2, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakuradani Kofun No.2 | map_type = Japan Toyama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|59.28|N|137|1|51.82|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Takaoka, Toyama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sakuradani Kofun Cluster'''|桜谷古墳群| Sakuradani Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ōta neighborhood of the city of [[Takaoka, Toyama]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. Two of the tumuli were designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216289 |title= 桜谷古墳群 |trans-title= Sakuradani kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakuradani Kofun Cluster of ''kofun'' is located on the margin of a hill at the base of Mount Futagami overlooking [[Toyama Bay]] in northwestern Toyama Prefecture. The site consisted of a ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) tumuli (No.1), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above and a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hotategaigata-kofun''|帆立貝型古墳||}}) tumuli (No.2) and ten or more smaller {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}) tumuli. The first was discovered in 1918 when a tree was being planted at a local [[Shinto shrine]], and nine more were discovered by 1924. Tombs No.1 and No.2 have not been surveyed internally in detail, but are presumed to date from the beginning of the fifth century in the late [[Kofun period]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These two tombs were protected as a National Historic Site since 1934; however, the remaining tombs were destroyed when the area was developed for agricultural land after [[World War II]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
In 1976, one more dome-shaped tumulus and two box-shaped stone sarcophagus were discovered during the construction of a prefectural road. However, during this same construction work and despite the National Historic Site status, a portion of Tomb No.2 was destroyed.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" |+ Sakuradani Kofun cluster |- ! Kofun !! type !! Length || Posterior || Anterior || Excavated |- | No.1 || keyhole || 62m || 35m diameter x 7m height || 30m width x 5.45 m height || unexcavated |- | No.2 || keyhole || 50m || 33m diameter x 6m height || ?m width x 1 m height || 1923, 1945 |- |}
Numerous [[grave good]]s uncovered during various [[archaeological excavations]], of which a 14-centimeter diameter [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] (possibly of Chinese origin), 13 cylindrical beads, three gold rings, two knives, 17 small beads and a jasper spindle are designated as Important Tangible Cultural Properties of Takaoka City. The mirror is kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and other artifacts at the Toyama City Museum. The site is about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Amaharashi Station|Amaharashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Himi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Toyama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Sakuradani Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.takaoka.toyama.jp/syoubun/kanko/rekishi/kinen/sakuradani.html Takaoka city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.pref.toyama.jp/sections/3009/3007/mb/mb002-23.htm Toyama Prefectural home page] {{in lang|ja}}
Sakuradani Kofun
{{Kofun navbar}}

[[Category:History of Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Takaoka, Toyama]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]]
[[sn:Sakuradani Kofun]]}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Sakuradani Kofun Cluster | native_name = 桜谷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakuradani Kofun Group (Takaoka)-2, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakuradani Kofun No.2 | map_type = Japan Toyama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|59.28|N|137|1|51.82|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Takaoka, Toyama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sakuradani Kofun Cluster'''|桜谷古墳群| Sakuradani Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ōta neighborhood of the city of [[Takaoka, Toyama]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. Two of the tumuli were designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216289 |title= 桜谷古墳群 |trans-title= Sakuradani kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakuradani Kofun Cluster of ''kofun'' is located on the margin of a hill at the base of Mount Futagami overlooking [[Toyama Bay]] in northwestern Toyama Prefecture. The site consisted of a ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) tumuli (No.1), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above and a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hotategaigata-kofun''|帆立貝型古墳||}}) tumuli (No.2) and ten or more smaller {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}) tumuli. The first was discovered in 1918 when a tree was being planted at a local [[Shinto shrine]], and nine more were discovered by 1924. Tombs No.1 and No.2 have not been surveyed internally in detail, but are presumed to date from the beginning of the fifth century in the late [[Kofun period]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These two tombs were protected as a National Historic Site since 1934; however, the remaining tombs were destroyed when the area was developed for agricultural land after [[World War II]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
In 1976, one more dome-shaped tumulus and two box-shaped stone sarcophagus were discovered during the construction of a prefectural road. However, during this same construction work and despite the National Historic Site status, a portion of Tomb No.2 was destroyed.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" |+ Sakuradani Kofun cluster |- ! Kofun !! type !! Length || Posterior || Anterior || Excavated |- | No.1 || keyhole || 62m || 35m diameter x 7m height || 30m width x 5.45 m height || unexcavated |- | No.2 || keyhole || 50m || 33m diameter x 6m height || ?m width x 1 m height || 1923, 1945 |- |}
Numerous [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s uncovered during various [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Archaeological excavations]], of which a 14-centimeter diameter [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] (possibly of Chinese origin), 13 cylindrical beads, three gold rings, two knives, 17 small beads and a jasper spindle are designated as Important Tangible Cultural Properties of Takaoka City. The mirror is kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and other artifacts at the Toyama City Museum. The site is about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Amaharashi Station|Amaharashi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[:en:Himi Line|Himi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Toyama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Sakuradani Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.takaoka.toyama.jp/syoubun/kanko/rekishi/kinen/sakuradani.html Takaoka city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.pref.toyama.jp/sections/3009/3007/mb/mb002-23.htm Toyama Prefectural home page] {{in lang|ja}}
Sakuradani Kofun

[[sn:Sakuradani Kofun]]}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove obsolete category Pages with valid en interwikis
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Sakuradani Kofun Cluster | native_name = 桜谷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakuradani Kofun Group (Takaoka)-2, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakuradani Kofun No.2 | map_type = Japan Toyama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|59.28|N|137|1|51.82|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Takaoka, Toyama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sakuradani Kofun Cluster'''|桜谷古墳群| Sakuradani Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ōta neighborhood of the city of [[Takaoka, Toyama]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Two of the tumuli were designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216289 |title= 桜谷古墳群 |trans-title= Sakuradani kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakuradani Kofun Cluster of ''kofun'' is located on the margin of a hill at the base of Mount Futagami overlooking [[Toyama Bay]] in northwestern Toyama Prefecture. The site consisted of a ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) tumuli (No.1), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above and a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hotategaigata-kofun''|帆立貝型古墳||}}) tumuli (No.2) and ten or more smaller {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}) tumuli. The first was discovered in 1918 when a tree was being planted at a local [[Shinto shrine]], and nine more were discovered by 1924. Tombs No.1 and No.2 have not been surveyed internally in detail, but are presumed to date from the beginning of the fifth century in the late [[Kofun period]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These two tombs were protected as a National Historic Site since 1934; however, the remaining tombs were destroyed when the area was developed for agricultural land after [[World War II]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
In 1976, one more dome-shaped tumulus and two box-shaped stone sarcophagus were discovered during the construction of a prefectural road. However, during this same construction work and despite the National Historic Site status, a portion of Tomb No.2 was destroyed.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" |+ Sakuradani Kofun cluster |- ! Kofun !! type !! Length || Posterior || Anterior || Excavated |- | No.1 || keyhole || 62m || 35m diameter x 7m height || 30m width x 5.45 m height || unexcavated |- | No.2 || keyhole || 50m || 33m diameter x 6m height || ?m width x 1 m height || 1923, 1945 |- |}
Numerous [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s uncovered during various [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Archaeological excavations]], of which a 14-centimeter diameter [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] (possibly of Chinese origin), 13 cylindrical beads, three gold rings, two knives, 17 small beads and a jasper spindle are designated as Important Tangible Cultural Properties of Takaoka City. The mirror is kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and other artifacts at the Toyama City Museum. The site is about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Amaharashi Station|Amaharashi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Himi Line|Himi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Toyama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Toyama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Sakuradani Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.takaoka.toyama.jp/syoubun/kanko/rekishi/kinen/sakuradani.html Takaoka city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.pref.toyama.jp/sections/3009/3007/mb/mb002-23.htm Toyama Prefectural home page] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Sakuradani Kofun Cluster | native_name = 桜谷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakuradani Kofun Group (Takaoka)-2, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakuradani Kofun No.2 | map_type = Japan Toyama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|59.28|N|137|1|51.82|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Takaoka, Toyama|Takaoka, Toyama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sakuradani Kofun Cluster'''|桜谷古墳群| Sakuradani Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ōta neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Takaoka, Toyama|Takaoka, Toyama]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Two of the tumuli were designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216289 |title= 桜谷古墳群 |trans-title= Sakuradani kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakuradani Kofun Cluster of ''kofun'' is located on the margin of a hill at the base of Mount Futagami overlooking [[Toyama Bay]] in northwestern Toyama Prefecture. The site consisted of a ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) tumuli (No.1), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above and a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hotategaigata-kofun''|帆立貝型古墳||}}) tumuli (No.2) and ten or more smaller {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}) tumuli. The first was discovered in 1918 when a tree was being planted at a local [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]], and nine more were discovered by 1924. Tombs No.1 and No.2 have not been surveyed internally in detail, but are presumed to date from the beginning of the fifth century in the late [[Kofun period]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These two tombs were protected as a National Historic Site since 1934; however, the remaining tombs were destroyed when the area was developed for agricultural land after [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
In 1976, one more dome-shaped tumulus and two box-shaped stone sarcophagus were discovered during the construction of a prefectural road. However, during this same construction work and despite the National Historic Site status, a portion of Tomb No.2 was destroyed.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" |+ Sakuradani Kofun cluster |- ! Kofun !! type !! Length || Posterior || Anterior || Excavated |- | No.1 || keyhole || 62m || 35m diameter x 7m height || 30m width x 5.45 m height || unexcavated |- | No.2 || keyhole || 50m || 33m diameter x 6m height || ?m width x 1 m height || 1923, 1945 |- |}
Numerous [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s uncovered during various [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Archaeological excavations]], of which a 14-centimeter diameter [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] (possibly of Chinese origin), 13 cylindrical beads, three gold rings, two knives, 17 small beads and a jasper spindle are designated as Important Tangible Cultural Properties of Takaoka City. The mirror is kept at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] and other artifacts at the Toyama City Museum. The site is about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Amaharashi Station|Amaharashi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Himi Line|Himi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Toyama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Toyama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Sakuradani Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.takaoka.toyama.jp/syoubun/kanko/rekishi/kinen/sakuradani.html Takaoka city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.pref.toyama.jp/sections/3009/3007/mb/mb002-23.htm Toyama Prefectural home page] {{in lang|ja}}

Tombs No.1 and No.2 have not been surveyed internally in detail, but are presumed to date from the beginning of the fifth century in the late [[Kofun period]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These two tombs were protected as a National Historic Site since 1934; however, the remaining tombs were destroyed when the area was developed for agricultural land after [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
In 1976, one more dome-shaped tumulus and two box-shaped stone sarcophagus were discovered during the construction of a prefectural road. However, during this same construction work and despite the National Historic Site status, a portion of Tomb No.2 was destroyed.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" |+ Sakuradani Kofun cluster |- ! Kofun !! type !! Length || Posterior || Anterior || Excavated |- | No.1 || keyhole || 62m || 35m diameter x 7m height || 30m width x 5.45 m height || unexcavated |- | No.2 || keyhole || 50m || 33m diameter x 6m height || ?m width x 1 m height || 1923, 1945 |- |}
Numerous [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s uncovered during various [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Archaeological excavations]], of which a 14-centimeter diameter [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] (possibly of Chinese origin), 13 cylindrical beads, three gold rings, two knives, 17 small beads and a jasper spindle are designated as Important Tangible Cultural Properties of Takaoka City. The mirror is kept at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] and other artifacts at the Toyama City Museum. The site is about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Amaharashi Station|Amaharashi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Himi Line|Himi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sakuradani Kofun Group]][[en:Sakuradani Kofun]][[ja:桜谷古墳群 (高岡市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17991834]] Wikidata name: Sakuradani Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Sakuradani Kofun Cluster | native_name = 桜谷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Sakuradani Kofun Group (Takaoka)-1, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakuradani Kofun No.2 | map_type = Japan Toyama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.81625|N|137.031944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Takaoka, Toyama|Takaoka, Toyama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Sakuradani Kofun Cluster'''|桜谷古墳群| Sakuradani Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Ōta neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Takaoka, Toyama|Takaoka, Toyama]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Two of the tumuli were designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216289 |title= 桜谷古墳群 |trans-title= Sakuradani kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakuradani Kofun Cluster of ''kofun'' is located on the margin of a hill at the base of Mount Futagami overlooking [[:en:Toyama Bay|Toyama Bay]] in northwestern Toyama Prefecture. The site consisted of a ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}) tumuli (No.1), which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above and a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hotategaigata-kofun''|帆立貝型古墳||}}) tumuli (No.2) and ten or more smaller {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}) tumuli. The first was discovered in 1918 when a tree was being planted at a local [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]], and nine more were discovered by 1924. Tombs No.1 and No.2 have not been surveyed internally in detail, but are presumed to date from the beginning of the fifth century in the late [[Kofun period]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These two tombs were protected as a National Historic Site since 1934; however, the remaining tombs were destroyed when the area was developed for agricultural land after [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
In 1976, one more dome-shaped tumulus and two box-shaped stone sarcophagus were discovered during the construction of a prefectural road. However, during this same construction work and despite the National Historic Site status, a portion of Tomb No.2 was destroyed.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" |+ Sakuradani Kofun cluster |- ! Kofun !! type !! Length || Posterior || Anterior || Excavated |- | No.1 || keyhole || 62m || 35m diameter x 7m height || 30m width x 5.45 m height || unexcavated |- | No.2 || keyhole || 50m || 33m diameter x 6m height || ?m width x 1 m height || 1923, 1945 |- |}
Numerous [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s uncovered during various [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Archaeological excavations]], of which a 14-centimeter diameter [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] (possibly of Chinese origin), 13 cylindrical beads, three gold rings, two knives, 17 small beads and a jasper spindle are designated as Important Tangible Cultural Properties of Takaoka City. The mirror is kept at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] and other artifacts at the Toyama City Museum. The site is about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Amaharashi Station|Amaharashi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Himi Line|Himi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Toyama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Toyama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Sakuradani Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.takaoka.toyama.jp/syoubun/kanko/rekishi/kinen/sakuradani.html Takaoka city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.pref.toyama.jp/sections/3009/3007/mb/mb002-23.htm Toyama Prefectural home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The first was discovered in 1918 when a tree was being planted at a local [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]], and nine more were discovered by 1924. Tombs No.1 and No.2 have not been surveyed internally in detail, but are presumed to date from the beginning of the fifth century in the late [[Kofun period]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
